prompt:
A small two-wheeled line-following robot sits on a white tabletop track made from a thick black line that curves gently across a sheet of paper or a foam board. The robot has a compact chassis with two drive wheels, a caster at the rear, and two downward-facing reflectance or light sensors mounted at the front, positioned directly over the dark track line. Thin wires run from the sensors back to a visible microcontroller board on top, with a small battery pack neatly strapped to the frame, making it obvious that the robot is built for following the contrasting line.

Set in a clean, near-future hobby workshop with soft, even lighting and a subtle solarpunk vibe: a few green plants, shelves with organized parts bins, and maybe a sunlit window with distant city rooftops. The style is photorealistic 512×512 with slightly stylized colors, no brand logos, no text on the track. The composition centers the robot and the curved black line so it reads clearly as a line-following training setup, with background details softly blurred.
